description: Admitted with acute decompensated heart failure. Bilateral oedema, dyspnoea at rest.
Weight reduced by 2kg following diuretic therapy. Oedema improving.
BNP 450 pg/mL (down from 820 on admission). Renal function stable.
Admitted for warfarin initiation following DVT diagnosis in left leg.
INR 1.4 — below therapeutic range. Dose adjusted to 3mg.
INR 2.3 — within therapeutic range (2.0–3.0). Continue current dose.
Admitted with poorly controlled Type 2 diabetes. HbA1c 11.2%. Starting insulin.
Blood glucose stabilising. Fasting glucose 8.2 mmol/L, down from 16.4 on admission.
Post-operative day 1 following right hip replacement surgery. Vitals stable.
Mobilised with walking frame for first time. Pain VAS 4/10. Wound clean and dry.
Pain VAS 2/10. Mobilising well. Wound healing satisfactory.
Admitted for elective physiotherapy programme following right knee osteoarthritis.
Initial physio assessment completed. ROM right knee 45 degrees. Programme started.
